name: codex-video-pipeline-plugin
description: Codex CLI plugin for AI video projects. Version 1.1.3 uses a clear marketplace wrapper layout: the outer codex-video-intake-plugin directory is the install package, and the real plugin lives under plugins/codex-video-pipeline-plugin. The official mainline runs a guided Stage 00-09 video production pipeline with real local provider execution and manifest-based evidence checks.

interface.shortDescription: Guided Stage 00-09 video pipeline with official entry flow and provider-backed execution.
interface.longDescription: A Codex CLI skill pack for an AI video production pipeline. The outer package is a local marketplace wrapper; the real plugin root is plugins/codex-video-pipeline-plugin. The official user path starts at $video-production-pipeline and advances through Stage 00-09 with provider execution, manifest validation, and creator-facing review gates.
defaultPrompt: Use $video-production-pipeline as the default user entry point. When the user invokes $video-production-pipeline or asks to start/continue the video pipeline, do not begin with repo preflight, contract summary, startup explanation, or cleanup narration. Immediately follow the official entry chain and surface the current formal Stage 00/01/... prompt or gate. On a blank runtime state, the first visible response must be the official Stage 00 Question 1 prompt, not a meta summary. Only switch into repo-development reading mode when the user explicitly asks to inspect, modify, implement, or review the plugin itself; in that case read CODEX_START_HERE.md first and then .codex/current-task-contract.md. Keep all outputs inside video_projects/<project_id>/. For Stages 05-09, never claim generated or delivered success unless required files exist, have non-zero size where applicable, and the corresponding manifest validates in final mode. Do not read deleted historical codex-first plan documents. The only allowed business-flow test is the supervisor-run entry test that starts from $video-production-pipeline.
